Which one is an adverb?
Which of the following sentences is correct?
options_html: Only once did I went there.
Only once did I go there.
Only once did I gone there.
correct_answer_html: Only once did I go there.

question_html: The expression ‘throw down the gauntlet’ means-
options_html: To offer help in a crisis

question_html: Make it passive: “The director cast him in the lead role.”
options_html: He was cast in the lead role by the director.
He was casted in the lead role by the director.
He is cast in the lead role by the director.
He has been casting in the lead role.
correct_answer_html: He was cast in the lead role by the director.

question_html: “Come here.” - Choose the correct indirect speech.
options_html: He told that I should come here.
He ordered that I should go there.
He told me to go there.
correct_answer_html: He told me to go there.

question_html: Make it affirmative: “No sooner had he reached the station than the train left.”
options_html: As soon as he had reached the station, the train had left.
As soon as he reached the station, the train left.
As soon as he reached the station, than the train left.
As soon as he was reaching the station, the train was leaving.
correct_answer_html: As soon as he reached the station, the train left.

question_html: _____ that Nahid was able to retire early.
“The man is always kind to me.”- What is the negative form of this sentence?
options_html: The man is ever rude to me.
The man is never rude to me.
The man is always rude to me.
The man is not never rude to me.
correct_answer_html: The man is never rude to me.

question_html: The boy said, “I am hungry.”- The indirect speech is-
options_html: The boy said he is hungry.
The boy said that he had been hungry.
The boy said that he was hungry.
The boy says that he had been hungry.
correct_answer_html: The boy said that he was hungry.

question_html: ‘Dog days’ means-
Among these sentences, which one contains ‘after’ as an adverb?
options_html: After ages will sing his glory.
They arrived after the train had left the station.

question_html: Make it passive: “Panic seized me.”
The indirect speech of the sentence: He said, "Let me have some milk."
options_html: He told that he let have some milk.
He said that he let have some milk.
He proposed that he might have some milk.
He asked that he let have some milk.
correct_answer_html: He proposed that he might have some milk.

question_html: Make it interrogative: “None but the brave deserves the fair.”
options_html: Who else deserves the fair but the brave?
Doesn’t anyone but the brave deserve the fair?
Doesn’t the brave deserve the fair?
Who deserves the fair but the brave?
correct_answer_html: Who deserves the fair but the brave?

question_html: The direct narration of the sentence: “He told me that he had killed the bird.”
options_html: He asked to me, “Why did you kill the bird?”
He said to me, “We did kill the bird.”
He said to me, “He had killed the bird.”
He said to me, “I killed the bird.”
correct_answer_html: He said to me, “I killed the bird.”
